title: Sizewell B - Inspection ID: 53822
text:
Sizewell B - Inspection ID: 53822

Executive summary

Date(s) of inspection:

January 2026

Aim of inspection

This was a planned inspection to judge the licensee's compliance against LC 26 (Control and supervision of operations) in order to gain confidence that the licensee is maintaining responsibility for, and control over, the activities on its site which impact nuclear safety.

LC26 requires the licensee to ensure that no operations are carried out which may affect safety except under the control and supervision of suitably qualified and experienced persons appointed for that purpose by the licensee.

Subject(s) of inspection

LC26 - Control and supervision of operations - Rating: Green

Key findings, inspector's opinions and reasons for judgement made

The inspection observed shift manager handover and start-of-shift briefing activities followed by tasks carried out by the site's maintenance and operations teams. I judged that personnel demonstrated appropriate oversight of plant activities, clear understanding of operational priorities, and effective communication of risks and expectations. Supervisory personnel provided direction consistent with the licensee's arrangements, and operational decisions were made with due regard to plant status and safety.

Within the operations team, I judged the shift handover, executive engagement and start‑of‑shift processes to have appropriate focus on plant condition, current safety issues and recent incidents. I observed an emergency diesel generator barring task for which supervision and briefing arrangements were applied proportionately to task risk, and operators demonstrated competent execution of duties, including effective use of human performance tools. Where new information or unexpected conditions arose, personnel responded promptly and appropriately. I judged that the licensee's arrangements were suitably implemented.

For the two maintenance teams, I observed clear and engaging start-of-shift and pre-job briefs with personnel demonstrating suitable awareness of equipment condition and vulnerabilities. I observed maintenance tasks on a guardline primary protection system and a motor generator set and judged the execution of the tasks to be adequately controlled and supervised, with evidence of effective risk management, challenge and procedural adherence. I verified that suitable competence management arrangements were in place to support the deployment of suitably qualified and experienced personnel.

Conclusion

Overall, based upon the sample during the inspection I was satisfied that Sizewell B is compliant with LC26 with regard to handovers, setting to work and control and supervision of operations at the workface for operations and maintenance teams. I am therefore content to rate LC26 as Green (no formal action). No regulatory issues were raised from this inspection.


                            Published
                        


                            17 March 2026
                        

Sizewell B


                                EDF
